children.text: Computation of benefits; application to aliens and nonnationals
The minimum limit on weekly compensation for disability, established by

children.tail: , and the minimum limit on the average weekly wages on which death benefits are to be computed, established by
, shall not apply in computing compensation and death benefits under this chapter.

children.text: Compensation for permanent total or permanent partial disability under

children.tail: , or for death under this chapter to aliens and nonnationals of the United States not residents of the United States or Canada shall be in the same amount as provided for residents, except that dependents in any foreign country shall be limited to surviving wife and child or children, or if there be no surviving wife or child or children, to surviving father or mother whom the employee has supported, either wholly or in part, for the period of one year immediately prior to the date of the injury, and except that the Secretary of Labor may, at his option or upon the application of the insurance carrier shall, commute all future installments of compensation to be paid to such aliens or nonnationals of the United States by paying or causing to be paid to them one-half of the commuted amount of such future installments of compensation as determined by the Secretary.

substituted references to sections of the Longshore and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act for sections of the Longshoremen’s and Harbor Workers’ Compensation Act, which references have been translated to sections of title 33, thus requiring no change in text.
, set out as a note under

children.text: “Secretary of Labor” and “Secretary” substituted for “Federal Security Administrator” and “Administrator”, respectively, in subsec. (b), pursuant to Reorg. Plan No. 19 of 1950, § 1, eff.

children.tail: , which transferred functions of Federal Security Administrator to Secretary of Labor.

children.text: Previously, “Federal Security Administrator” and “Administrator” substituted for “United States Employees’ Compensation Commission” and “Commission” pursuant to Reorg. Plan No. 2 of 1946, § 3, eff.

children.tail: , which abolished United States Employees’ Compensation Commission and transferred its functions to Federal Security Administrator.
